lem:unit-fe-collapse.proof_tex:
Proof. Put c_{gt}=exp(gamma_{t0}+D_{gt}delta_{gt}). From the definition of the observed outcome, ass:unit-untreated-exponential-mean, ass:proportional-effects, and ass:irreversible-adoption, E[Y_{it}]=b_{iN}c_{G_i,t}=m_{itN}(delta). Hence units in the same cohort have proportional mean paths.

Write the normalized unit-and-time fixed-effect index as A_i+lambda_t+beta D_{gt}, with lambda_1=0, and the collapsed index as A_g+lambda_t+beta D_{gt}. These are nonsingular reparameterizations of v_{itN}'theta_N and r_{gt}'theta. Fix (lambda,beta), put z_{gt}=lambda_t+beta D_{gt}, C_g=sum_t c_{gt}>0, and H_g(lambda,beta)=sum_t exp(z_{gt})>0. For i in I_{gN}, the part of the unscaled unit criterion depending on A_i has first two derivatives

  d ell_i/dA_i=b_{iN}C_g-exp(A_i)H_g,
  d^2 ell_i/dA_i^2=-exp(A_i)H_g<0.

Its unique maximizer is A_i(lambda,beta)=log b_{iN}+log(C_g/H_g). Thus A_i-log b_{iN} is common within g at every profiled optimum.

Let h_{iN}=b_{iN}/bar b_{gN} for i in I_{gN}. Positivity makes this well-defined and sum_{i in I_{gN}}h_{iN}=n_{gN}. Given collapsed effects A_g, set A_i=A_g+log h_{iN}. With eta_{gt}=A_g+lambda_t+beta D_{gt}, one has m_{itN}=h_{iN}m_{gtN} and exp(A_i+lambda_t+beta D_{gt})=h_{iN}exp(eta_{gt}). Direct summation gives

  L_N((A_i),lambda,beta;delta)=L_Nc((A_g),lambda,beta;delta)+K_N(delta),

where

  K_N(delta)=(NT)^{-1}sum_{g,t}sum_{i in I_{gN}}h_{iN}m_{gtN}(delta)log h_{iN}

is independent of (A_g,lambda,beta). Indeed, the parameter-dependent terms in cell (g,t) equal

  (NT)^{-1}sum_{i in I_{gN}}h_{iN}{m_{gtN}eta_{gt}-exp(eta_{gt})}
  =q_{gtN}{m_{gtN}eta_{gt}-exp(eta_{gt})}.

Conversely, the preceding one-dimensional first-order condition shows that every unit-effect profile maximizer has this lifted form: the collapsed maximizer over A_g at fixed (lambda,beta) is A_g=log bar b_{gN}+log(C_g/H_g), so A_i=A_g+log h_{iN}. The two criteria therefore have identical profiled objectives in (lambda,beta), up to K_N, and their beta maximizers coincide.

Both maxima exist and are unique. The Hessian of L_N is

  -(NT)^{-1}sum_{i,t}exp(v_{itN}'theta_N)v_{itN}v_{itN}',

which is negative definite by ass:unit-fe-design-rank. The criterion is coercive. To see this, for any sequence with ||theta_l|| tending to infinity, pass to a subsequence u_l=theta_l/||theta_l|| tending to u. The design rows span, so some v_{itN}'u is nonzero. If one limiting inner product is positive, its negative exponential dominates the O(||theta_l||) linear part. If all are nonpositive, at least one is strictly negative, and the positive-mean linear part has a strictly negative order-||theta_l|| limit; the rows with zero limiting slope contribute only o(||theta_l||) to its positive part, while all exponential terms are nonpositive. Thus L_N(theta_l) tends to minus infinity. The same argument applies to L_Nc: ass:collapsed-design-rank says the fixed set {r_{gt}} spans, and every q_{gtN},m_{gtN} is positive. Strict concavity and coercivity give one maximizer for each criterion, proving the exact finite-N equality of beta coordinates.

For convergence, ass:cohort-share-limit and ass:within-cohort-baseline-limit give q_{gtN}->q_{gt} and m_{gtN}(delta)->m_{gt}(delta) cell by cell. Hence L_Nc converges to L uniformly on compact sets. Its maximizers theta_N^c are bounded. Otherwise normalize an unbounded subsequence as above. Because the finite collection of q_{gtN} and m_{gtN} is eventually bounded above and bounded away from zero, the same two-case argument forces L_Nc(theta_N^c)->-infinity, contradicting L_Nc(theta_N^c)>=L_Nc(0)=-1. Every convergent subsequence therefore has a limit theta_bar. Optimality and compact-uniform convergence imply L(theta_bar)>=L(theta) for every fixed theta. The limiting criterion is strictly concave and coercive under ass:collapsed-design-rank, so theta_bar=theta_star(delta). Thus theta_N^c->theta_star(delta) and beta_Nstar(delta)->beta_star(delta). The exact identity also shows that all within-cohort variation in b_{iN} occurs only in the parameter-free K_N; the optimizer depends on it only through bar b_{gN}, equivalently B_{gtN}=bar b_{gN}exp(gamma_{t0}). QED.

snapshot.stmt: For every N, the unit-and-time-FE population projection in def:unit-fe-population-projection has a unique beta_Nstar(delta). Its beta_Nstar(delta) equals the beta coordinate of the unique maximizer of L_Nc(theta;delta). Consequently, beta_Nstar(delta) converges to beta_star(delta). Thus within-cohort baseline heterogeneity (b_iN) enters the limiting unit-FE coefficient only through B_gtN=n_gN^(-1)sum_{i in I_gN}b_iN exp(gamma_t0).

defs.def:unit-fe-population-projection: beta_Nstar(delta) is the last coordinate of argmax_{theta_N in R^(N+T)} L_N(theta_N;delta), where L_N(theta_N;delta)=(NT)^(-1)sum_{i=1}^Nsum_{t=1}^T[m_itN(delta)v_itN'theta_N-exp(v_itN'theta_N)].
defs.def:collapsed-population-projection: theta_star(delta)=argmax_{theta in R^(|C|+T)}L(theta;delta), L(theta;delta)=sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^Tq_gt[m_gt(delta)r_gt'theta-exp(r_gt'theta)], beta_star(delta)=the last coordinate of theta_star(delta), and mu_star_gt(delta)=exp(r_gt'theta_star(delta)).

assumptions.ass:cohort-share-limit: For every N>=|C| and every g in C, n_gN is positive and n_gN/N converges to pi_g in (0,1).
assumptions.ass:unit-untreated-exponential-mean: E_Pcal_N[Y_it(0)]=b_iN exp(gamma_t0) for every i and t, with gamma_10=0.
assumptions.ass:within-cohort-baseline-limit: For every g in C, the deterministic triangular-array average bar_b_gN=n_gN^(-1)sum_{i in I_gN}b_iN converges to bar_b_g in (0,infinity).
assumptions.ass:proportional-effects: E_Pcal_N[Y_it(1)]=E_Pcal_N[Y_it(0)] exp(delta_{G_i,t}) for every i and t with D_it=1.
assumptions.ass:unit-fe-design-rank: For every N, (NT)^(-1)sum_{i=1}^Nsum_{t=1}^T v_itN v_itN' is positive definite.
assumptions.ass:collapsed-design-rank: sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^T q_gt r_gt r_gt' is positive definite.

lem:pseudo-true-ppml-projection.proof_tex:
Proof. For every theta,

  nabla L(theta;delta)=sum_{g,t}q_{gt}r_{gt}[m_{gt}(delta)-exp(r_{gt}'theta)],
  nabla^2 L(theta;delta)=-sum_{g,t}q_{gt}exp(r_{gt}'theta)r_{gt}r_{gt}'.

For nonzero a,

  -a'nabla^2L(theta;delta)a=sum_{g,t}q_{gt}exp(r_{gt}'theta)(r_{gt}'a)^2>0,

because all weights and fitted means are positive and ass:collapsed-design-rank says the r_{gt}'s span the parameter space. Thus L is strictly concave.

It also attains its maximum. If ||theta_l|| tends to infinity, pass to a subsequence u_l=theta_l/||theta_l|| tending to u. Spanning gives some r_{gt}'u nonzero. If max_{g,t}r_{gt}'u>0, one negative exponential term tends to minus infinity exponentially and dominates the finite O(||theta_l||) sum of linear terms. If all r_{gt}'u<=0, at least one is strictly negative; the positive-weight, positive-m_{gt} linear part has a strictly negative order-||theta_l|| limit, rows with zero limiting slope contribute only o(||theta_l||) to its positive part, and all negative exponential terms are nonpositive. Hence L(theta_l;delta)->-infinity. Therefore L is coercive and continuous, so it has a maximizer; strict concavity makes it unique, namely theta_star(delta). Interiority in Euclidean space gives

  0=nabla L(theta_star;delta)
   =sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^Tq_{gt}r_{gt}[m_{gt}(delta)-mu_star_{gt}(delta)].

This is the claimed score system. QED.

snapshot.stmt: The limiting projection theta_star(delta) is unique and satisfies sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^Tq_gt r_gt[m_gt(delta)-mu_star_gt(delta)]=0.

defs.def:collapsed-population-projection: theta_star(delta)=argmax_{theta in R^(|C|+T)}L(theta;delta), L(theta;delta)=sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^Tq_gt[m_gt(delta)r_gt'theta-exp(r_gt'theta)], beta_star(delta)=the last coordinate of theta_star(delta), and mu_star_gt(delta)=exp(r_gt'theta_star(delta)).

assumptions.ass:collapsed-design-rank: sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^T q_gt r_gt r_gt' is positive definite.

thm:sharp-ppml-forbidden-sign.proof_tex:
Proof. By lem:unit-fe-collapse, beta_star is the limit of the actual unit-and-time-fixed-effect population PPML coefficient. Define

  F(theta,delta)=sum_{g,t}q_{gt}r_{gt}[m_{gt}(delta)-exp(r_{gt}'theta)].

At theta_star this score is zero by lem:pseudo-true-ppml-projection. Let

  H=sum_{g,t}q_{gt}mu_star_{gt}r_{gt}r_{gt}'.

For nonzero a, a'Ha=sum q_{gt}mu_star_{gt}(a'r_{gt})^2>0 because all weights are positive and ass:collapsed-design-rank makes the r_{gt}'s span. Thus H is positive definite. The implicit-function theorem applies because partial F/partial theta=-H, so theta_star is continuously differentiable in every effect coordinate. For a treated cell (k,s),

  partial m_{gt}/partial delta_{ks}=1{(g,t)=(k,s)}B_{ks}exp(delta_{ks}).

Differentiating F(theta_star(delta),delta)=0 yields

  partial theta_star/partial delta_{ks}=H^{-1}q_{ks}r_{ks}B_{ks}exp(delta_{ks}).

Partition r_{gt}=(X_{gt}',D_{gt})' and write

  H=[A b; b' c],
  A=sum q mu XX',  b=sum q mu XD,  c=sum q mu D^2,

where every sum is over (g,t) and mu=mu_star(delta). The principal block A is positive definite. The projection in def:weighted-fwl-residual therefore has rho_star=A^{-1}b and Wtilde_{gt}=D_{gt}-X_{gt}'A^{-1}b. Its weighted residual sum of squares is the Schur complement

  S=c-b'A^{-1}b=sum_{g,t}q_{gt}mu_star_{gt}Wtilde_{gt}^2.

Positive definiteness of H gives S>0. Equivalently, S=0 would make D equal X'rho_star on every positive-weight cell, contradicting full column rank of r. The last row of H^{-1} is S^{-1}(-b'A^{-1},1), and hence

  e_beta'H^{-1}r_{ks}=S^{-1}[D_{ks}-b'A^{-1}X_{ks}]=Wtilde_{ks}/S.

Taking the last coordinate of the differentiated score gives

  d beta_star/d delta_{ks}
  =q_{ks}B_{ks}exp(delta_{ks})Wtilde_{ks}(delta)
   /{sum_{g,t}q_{gt}mu_star_{gt}(delta)Wtilde_{gt}(delta)^2}.

The denominator and every factor outside Wtilde_{ks} are strictly positive. The derivative therefore has the sign of Wtilde_{ks}; it is negative exactly when Wtilde_{ks}<0, which is precisely the forbidden-comparison condition. Finally, the projection weights are q_{gt}mu_star_{gt}(delta), so Wtilde, and potentially its sign, depend on the full effect vector through the pseudo-true fitted means. QED.

snapshot.stmt: For the limiting coefficient beta_star(delta) of the unit-and-time-FE Poisson projection, at every delta satisfying the assumptions and for every treated cell (k,s), beta_star(delta) is differentiable in delta_ks and d beta_star(delta)/d delta_ks=q_ks B_ks exp(delta_ks) Wtilde_ks(delta)/[sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^Tq_gt mu_star_gt(delta)Wtilde_gt(delta)^2]. The denominator is strictly positive. Hence sign{d beta_star(delta)/d delta_ks}=sign{Wtilde_ks(delta)}, and a treated cell is a forbidden comparison cell if and only if Wtilde_ks(delta)<0. Both Wtilde and its sign depend on delta through mu_star.

defs.def:collapsed-population-projection: theta_star(delta)=argmax_{theta in R^(|C|+T)}L(theta;delta), L(theta;delta)=sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^Tq_gt[m_gt(delta)r_gt'theta-exp(r_gt'theta)], beta_star(delta)=the last coordinate of theta_star(delta), and mu_star_gt(delta)=exp(r_gt'theta_star(delta)).
defs.def:weighted-fwl-residual: rho_star(delta)=argmin_{rho in R^(|C|+T-1)}sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^Tq_gt mu_star_gt(delta)[D_gt-X_gt'rho]^2 and Wtilde_gt(delta)=D_gt-X_gt'rho_star(delta).

assumptions.ass:collapsed-design-rank: sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^T q_gt r_gt r_gt' is positive definite.

prop:four-cohort-sign-reversal.proof_tex:
Proof. The limiting primitive tuple induced by W4 has pi_g=1/4, bar b_g=1, and B_{gt}=1. Every treated effect is positive, and log 4>log(101/100), so (2,4) is uniquely largest. The normalized row and column dummy design is full rank. Moreover, the two-way residual table calculated below is nonzero, so D is not in the nuisance span; hence the full collapsed design has rank.

A sharper calculation places W4 relative to the exact operational frontier. Consider the equal-share, unit-baseline family with common treated multiplier x>1 except multiplier y>1 in cell (2,4). In row order (2,3,4,infinity) and column order (1,2,3,4),

  m=[[1,x,x,y],[1,1,x,x],[1,1,1,x],[1,1,1,1]].

Let R_g=sum_t m_{gt}, C_t=sum_g m_{gt}, and M=sum_g R_g. At beta=0, the nuisance score equations uniquely fit mu^0_{gt}=R_gC_t/M, because this positive row-column multiplicative array matches every observed row and column margin. Here

  R=(1+2x+y,2+2x,3+x,4),
  C=(4,3+x,2+2x,1+2x+y),
  M=10+5x+y.

The observed treated total is 5x+y, whereas the numerator of the fitted treated total is

  F=R_2(C_2+C_3+C_4)+R_3(C_3+C_4)+R_4C_4
   =20x^2+10xy+38x+y^2+12y+15.

Since q_{gt}=1/16, the treatment score at this beta-zero conditional fit is

  s_0=(1/16){5x+y-F/M}
     ={5x^2+12x-2y-15}/{16M}.

The criterion is strictly concave by the just-verified full rank. If theta^0 is the conditional optimizer at beta=0 and theta_star is the full optimizer, strict monotonicity of its gradient gives, whenever s_0 is nonzero,

  0>(theta_star-theta^0)'[nabla L(theta_star)-nabla L(theta^0)]
   =-beta_star s_0.

Thus sign(beta_star)=sign(s_0); if s_0=0, theta^0 is stationary in every coordinate and is the unique maximizer. Therefore the exact zero frontier is

  y=(5x^2+12x-15)/2,

and beta_star<0 exactly above it. At x=101/100 the cutoff is 4441/4000, whereas W4 sets y=4. In fact its denominator-cleared frontier value is (5x^2+12x-2y-15)/16=-11559/32000<0. This both proves beta_star<0 and shows that y=4 is a valid but deliberately nonminimal witness. All restrictions defining R_4 are satisfied, so the limiting tuple induced by W4 belongs to R_4.

For the diagnostic calculation, use the auxiliary no-effect vector delta^0 with every treated coordinate zero. Then m_{gt}=mu_star_{gt}=1 and beta_star=0. Equal q mu weights make Wtilde the ordinary two-way residual of D. The row means of D are (3/4,1/2,1/4,0), its column means are (0,1/4,1/2,3/4), and its grand mean is 3/8. Hence

  Wtilde(delta^0)=(1/8)[[-3,3,1,-1],[-1,-3,3,1],[1,-1,-3,3],[3,1,-1,-3]].

In particular Wtilde_{2,4}=-1/8. Also sum qWtilde^2=5/64 and q_{2,4}Wtilde_{2,4}=-1/128, so thm:sharp-ppml-forbidden-sign gives

  (partial beta_star/partial delta_{2,4})|_{delta^0}=-1/10<0.

The implicit-function argument in that theorem makes this derivative continuous. Thus it remains negative on a ball around zero. If K is the number of treated cells, choose a vector bar delta=(epsilon,...,epsilon) in that ball with epsilon>0 and then a sufficiently small ball around bar delta contained both in the strict positive orthant and in the original ball. This is a genuine open all-positive-effect neighborhood on which increasing the late effect of the earliest cohort strictly lowers beta_star. QED.

snapshot.stmt: The explicitly constructed W4 belongs to R_4. In particular, every treated cell has a strictly positive proportional effect, the exceptional cell (2,4) has the largest effect, and beta_star(delta)<0. At b_iN=1 and delta_gt=0 for all treated cells, Wtilde_gt equals the matrix with rows g=2,3,4,infinity and columns t=1,2,3,4: [(-3,3,1,-1),(-1,-3,3,1),(1,-1,-3,3),(3,1,-1,-3)]/8. Thus Wtilde_2,4=-1/8 and d beta_star(delta)/d delta_2,4<0 at the homogeneous no-effect point; continuity gives an open positive-effect neighborhood in which increasing the late effect of the earliest cohort strictly lowers beta_star.

defs.def:four-cohort-witness: W4 is the cofinal triangular subsequence indexed by N=4m, m=1,2,..., with T=4, C={2,3,4,infinity}, n_2N=n_3N=n_4N=n_infinityN=m, b_iN=1 for every i, gamma_t0=0 for every t, delta_gt=log(101/100) for every treated cell except delta_2,4=log(4).
defs.def:global-sign-reversal-region: R_T={((pi_g)_{g in C},(B_gt)_{g in C,1<=t<=T},(delta_gt)_{(g,t):D_gt=1}): C contains {2,3,4,infinity}; B_gt=bar_b_g exp(gamma_t0); delta_gt>0 whenever D_gt=1; sum_{g,t}q_gt r_gt r_gt' is positive definite; and beta_star(delta)<0}.
defs.def:weighted-fwl-residual: rho_star(delta)=argmin_{rho in R^(|C|+T-1)}sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^Tq_gt mu_star_gt(delta)[D_gt-X_gt'rho]^2 and Wtilde_gt(delta)=D_gt-X_gt'rho_star(delta).

prop:homogeneous-effect-reduction.proof_tex:
Proof. Suppose delta_{gt}=delta_0 in every treated cell. Since D_{gt} is binary,

  m_{gt}(delta)=B_{gt}exp(D_{gt}delta_0)
              =exp{log bar b_g+gamma_{t0}+D_{gt}delta_0}.

This log mean lies exactly in the collapsed design. Choose the normalized parameter theta_0 with intercept log bar b_infinity, cohort-g coefficient log bar b_g-log bar b_infinity for g not equal to infinity, time-t coefficient gamma_{t0} for t>=2, and treatment coefficient delta_0. Then r_{gt}'theta_0=log m_{gt}(delta) in every cell.

For fixed m>0, f_m(eta)=m eta-exp(eta) is strictly concave and uniquely maximized at eta=log m. Therefore, cell by cell,

  L(theta;delta)<=sum_{g,t}q_{gt}[m_{gt}log m_{gt}-m_{gt}]=L(theta_0;delta),

with equality only if r_{gt}'theta=log m_{gt} for every cell. By ass:collapsed-design-rank the r_{gt}'s span, so equality implies theta=theta_0. Hence theta_star=theta_0, beta_star(delta)=delta_0, and

  mu_star_{gt}(delta)=exp(r_{gt}'theta_0)=B_{gt}exp(D_{gt}delta_0).

By lem:unit-fe-collapse this is also the limiting individual-unit-and-time-FE projection. QED.

snapshot.stmt: If delta_gt=delta_0 for every treated cell, then beta_star(delta)=delta_0 and mu_star_gt(delta)=B_gt exp(D_gt delta_0).

defs.def:collapsed-population-projection: theta_star(delta)=argmax_{theta in R^(|C|+T)}L(theta;delta), L(theta;delta)=sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^Tq_gt[m_gt(delta)r_gt'theta-exp(r_gt'theta)], beta_star(delta)=the last coordinate of theta_star(delta), and mu_star_gt(delta)=exp(r_gt'theta_star(delta)).

assumptions.ass:unit-untreated-exponential-mean: E_Pcal_N[Y_it(0)]=b_iN exp(gamma_t0) for every i and t, with gamma_10=0.
assumptions.ass:collapsed-design-rank: sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^T q_gt r_gt r_gt' is positive definite.

thm:primitive-global-frontier.proof_tex:
Proof. Part I: exact primitive frontier. All h_{gt}, R_g, C_t, and M are strictly positive. Since q_{gt}=pi_g/T and m_{gt}=h_{gt}/pi_g, constrain beta to zero and define

  mu^0_{gt}=R_g C_t/(pi_g M).

This array is representable by intercept, cohort, and time effects because

  log mu^0_{gt}=log(R_g/pi_g)+log C_t-log M.

It matches every nuisance margin. Indeed, using sum_t C_t=M and sum_g R_g=M,

  sum_t pi_g mu^0_{gt}=R_g=sum_t pi_g m_{gt},
  sum_g pi_g mu^0_{gt}=C_t=sum_g pi_g m_{gt}.

After division by T these are exactly all cohort, time, and intercept score equations. The nuisance block is full rank as a principal block of the full collapsed Gram matrix, so this conditional fit is unique. Let theta^0=(eta^0,0) denote its normalized coefficient. Its only possibly nonzero score is the treatment coordinate

  s_0=sum_{g,t}q_{gt}D_{gt}(m_{gt}-mu^0_{gt})
     =(1/T){A-(1/M)sum_{g,t}D_{gt}R_gC_t}
     =Phi/(TM).

No pseudo-true nuisance multiplier remains in this expression. The Hessian of L is

  nabla^2 L(theta)=-sum_{g,t}q_{gt}exp(r_{gt}'theta)r_{gt}r_{gt}',

which is negative definite under ass:collapsed-design-rank. Hence L is strictly concave and its gradient is strictly monotone: for distinct theta_1 and theta_0,

  (theta_1-theta_0)'[nabla L(theta_1)-nabla L(theta_0)]<0.

If s_0=0, theta^0 is stationary in every coordinate and strict concavity makes it theta_star, so beta_star=0. If s_0 is nonzero, theta_star differs from theta^0 and

  0>(theta_star-theta^0)'[nabla L(theta_star)-nabla L(theta^0)]
   =-beta_star s_0,

because nabla L(theta_star)=0 and the nuisance coordinates of nabla L(theta^0) vanish. Thus beta_star s_0>0. Conversely, beta_star=0 with s_0 nonzero would contradict the same strict inequality. Since TM>0,

  sign{beta_star(delta)}=sign(s_0)=sign(Phi).

Phi is a finite explicit expression in pi_g, B_{gt}, and exp(D_gt delta_gt). Equality Phi=0 is equivalent to the two weak inequalities Phi>=0 and -Phi>=0. Intersecting Phi<0 with the primitive restrictions in def:global-sign-reversal-region gives exactly R_T. By lem:unit-fe-collapse this is a frontier for the limiting naive individual-unit-and-time-FE PPML coefficient, not merely for a relabelled cohort regression.

Part II: operational T=4 threshold. In the equal-share, unit-baseline family from the statement, the observed mean matrix, in row order (2,3,4,infinity) and column order (1,2,3,4), is

  [[1,x,x,y],[1,1,x,x],[1,1,1,x],[1,1,1,1]].

Its unweighted row margins are (1+2x+y,2+2x,3+x,4), its unweighted column margins are (4,3+x,2+2x,1+2x+y), its total is 10+5x+y, and its treated total is 5x+y. Direct expansion gives

  sum_{g,t}D_{gt}R_gC_t
   =20x^2+10xy+38x+y^2+12y+15

after removal of the common share factors. Because pi_g=1/4, every product in Phi carries the common factor 1/16. Therefore

  Phi=[(10+5x+y)(5x+y)
       -(20x^2+10xy+38x+y^2+12y+15)]/16
      =(5x^2+12x-2y-15)/16.

Part I yields beta_star<0 exactly when

  y>(5x^2+12x-15)/2,

and equality is the exact zero frontier. At x=101/100 this cutoff is 4441/4000. Moreover

  (5x^2+12x-15)/2-x=(5/2)(x-1)(x+3)>0

for x>1, so reversal forces the exceptional multiplier to be uniquely largest. W4 takes x=101/100 and y=4, for which Phi=-11559/32000<0; thus its witness is valid but nonminimal.

Part III: diagnose, then target causal PTT. Let H={(g,t):D_{gt}=1}. Since every adoption cohort belongs to {2,...,T,infinity}, D_{g1}=0. The period-one normalization and the limiting untreated mean give

  m_{g1}=B_{g1}=bar b_g.

The never-treated cohort obeys D_{infinity,t}=0 for all t, so

  m_{infinity,t}=bar b_infinity exp(gamma_{t0}),
  m_{infinity,1}=bar b_infinity>0.

Consequently the observed-population counterfactual baseline

  Bobs_{gt}=m_{g1}m_{infinity,t}/m_{infinity,1}

equals bar b_g exp(gamma_{t0})=B_{gt}. In a treated cell, proportional effects give m_{gt}=B_{gt}exp(delta_{gt}); hence

  tau_{gt}=m_{gt}/Bobs_{gt}-1=exp(delta_{gt})-1.

Positive cohort shares and baselines imply Z=sum_H q_{gt}Bobs_{gt}>0, omega_{gt}=q_{gt}Bobs_{gt}/Z>0, and sum_H omega_{gt}=1. Therefore

  PTT=sum_H omega_{gt}tau_{gt}
     ={sum_H q_{gt}B_{gt}exp(delta_{gt})}/{sum_H q_{gt}B_{gt}}-1
     ={sum_H q_{gt}m_{gt}}/{sum_H q_{gt}Bobs_{gt}}-1.

This is a computable positive-counterfactual-share aggregation of the identified granular proportional effects. If every delta_{gt}>0 on H, then every tau_{gt}>0 and PTT>0. Part I says Phi<0 exactly when beta_star<0. Hence Phi<0 diagnoses the pooled PPML coefficient as an unsafe causal summary: the recommended granular effects and PTT target a distinct causal estimand and do not algebraically correct or reinterpret beta_star. Everything here is an observed-population statement; no fixed-T sampling limit, influence function, variance formula, or coverage result for hat_beta_N is asserted. QED.

snapshot.stmt: For h_gt=pi_g B_gt exp(D_gt delta_gt), define R_g=sum_{t=1}^T h_gt, C_t=sum_{g in C}h_gt, M=sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^T h_gt, A=sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^T D_gt h_gt, and Phi=M A-sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^T D_gt R_g C_t. Then sign{beta_star(delta)}=sign(Phi). In particular beta_star(delta)=0 if and only if Phi=0, so the requested zero-level set is the finite primitive system Phi>=0 and -Phi>=0, with no pseudo-true intercept, cohort, or time multiplier retained; subject to the defining positive-effect, baseline, share, and rank restrictions, R_T is exactly Phi<0. In the T=4, C={2,3,4,infinity}, equal-share, unit-baseline subfamily having treated multiplier x>1 outside (2,4) and multiplier y>1 at (2,4), Phi=(5x^2+12x-2y-15)/16, so beta_star<0 if and only if y>(5x^2+12x-15)/2; at x=101/100 the sharp cutoff is 4441/4000, and W4 has y=4. Operationally, let H={(g,t):D_gt=1}, Bobs_gt=m_g1 m_infinity,t/m_infinity,1, Z=sum_H q_gt Bobs_gt, omega_gt=q_gt Bobs_gt/Z, tau_gt=m_gt/Bobs_gt-1, and PTT=sum_H omega_gt tau_gt. Under the multiplicative-parallel-trends and proportional-effect assumptions, Bobs_gt=B_gt, tau_gt=exp(delta_gt)-1, every omega_gt is positive and the weights sum to one, and PTT=(sum_H q_gt m_gt)/(sum_H q_gt Bobs_gt)-1. Thus with positive effects, Phi<0 implies beta_star<0<PTT: Phi diagnoses the pooled PPML coefficient as an unsafe causal summary, while granular tau_gt and their counterfactual-share PTT form a distinct identified causal target, not a correction or reinterpretation of beta_star. This population result makes no fixed-T sampling or inference claim for hat_beta_N.

defs.def:global-sign-reversal-region: R_T={((pi_g)_{g in C},(B_gt)_{g in C,1<=t<=T},(delta_gt)_{(g,t):D_gt=1}): C contains {2,3,4,infinity}; B_gt=bar_b_g exp(gamma_t0); delta_gt>0 whenever D_gt=1; sum_{g,t}q_gt r_gt r_gt' is positive definite; and beta_star(delta)<0}.
defs.def:frontier-elimination-handle: Starting from the limiting collapsed score equations sum_{g,t}q_gt X_gt[m_gt(delta)-mu_star_gt(delta)]=0 and sum_{g,t}q_gt D_gt[m_gt(delta)-mu_star_gt(delta)]=0, eliminate the intercept, cohort, and time multipliers by their fitted row and column margins, then characterize the beta_star(delta)=0 zero-level set in pi_g=lim_N n_gN/N, B_gt, and delta_gt.
defs.def:collapsed-population-projection: theta_star(delta)=argmax_{theta in R^(|C|+T)}L(theta;delta), L(theta;delta)=sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^Tq_gt[m_gt(delta)r_gt'theta-exp(r_gt'theta)], beta_star(delta)=the last coordinate of theta_star(delta), and mu_star_gt(delta)=exp(r_gt'theta_star(delta)).
defs.def:four-cohort-witness: W4 is the cofinal triangular subsequence indexed by N=4m, m=1,2,..., with T=4, C={2,3,4,infinity}, n_2N=n_3N=n_4N=n_infinityN=m, b_iN=1 for every i, gamma_t0=0 for every t, delta_gt=log(101/100) for every treated cell except delta_2,4=log(4).

assumptions.ass:cohort-share-limit: For every N>=|C| and every g in C, n_gN is positive and n_gN/N converges to pi_g in (0,1).
assumptions.ass:unit-untreated-exponential-mean: E_Pcal_N[Y_it(0)]=b_iN exp(gamma_t0) for every i and t, with gamma_10=0.
assumptions.ass:within-cohort-baseline-limit: For every g in C, the deterministic triangular-array average bar_b_gN=n_gN^(-1)sum_{i in I_gN}b_iN converges to bar_b_g in (0,infinity).
assumptions.ass:proportional-effects: E_Pcal_N[Y_it(1)]=E_Pcal_N[Y_it(0)] exp(delta_{G_i,t}) for every i and t with D_it=1.
assumptions.ass:collapsed-design-rank: sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^T q_gt r_gt r_gt' is positive definite.

node.statement: For h_gt=pi_g B_gt exp(D_gt delta_gt), define R_g=sum_{t=1}^T h_gt, C_t=sum_{g in C}h_gt, M=sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^T h_gt, A=sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^T D_gt h_gt, and Phi=M A-sum_{g in C}sum_{t=1}^T D_gt R_g C_t. Then sign{beta_star(delta)}=sign(Phi). In particular beta_star(delta)=0 if and only if Phi=0, so the requested zero-level set is the finite primitive system Phi>=0 and -Phi>=0, with no pseudo-true intercept, cohort, or time multiplier retained; subject to the defining positive-effect, baseline, share, and rank restrictions, R_T is exactly Phi<0. In the T=4, C={2,3,4,infinity}, equal-share, unit-baseline subfamily having treated multiplier x>1 outside (2,4) and multiplier y>1 at (2,4), Phi=(5x^2+12x-2y-15)/16, so beta_star<0 if and only if y>(5x^2+12x-15)/2; at x=101/100 the sharp cutoff is 4441/4000, and W4 has y=4. Operationally, let H={(g,t):D_gt=1}, Bobs_gt=m_g1 m_infinity,t/m_infinity,1, Z=sum_H q_gt Bobs_gt, omega_gt=q_gt Bobs_gt/Z, tau_gt=m_gt/Bobs_gt-1, and PTT=sum_H omega_gt tau_gt. Under the multiplicative-parallel-trends and proportional-effect assumptions, Bobs_gt=B_gt, tau_gt=exp(delta_gt)-1, every omega_gt is positive and the weights sum to one, and PTT=(sum_H q_gt m_gt)/(sum_H q_gt Bobs_gt)-1. Thus with positive effects, Phi<0 implies beta_star<0<PTT: Phi diagnoses the pooled PPML coefficient as an unsafe causal summary, while granular tau_gt and their counterfactual-share PTT form a distinct identified causal target, not a correction or reinterpretation of beta_star. This population result makes no fixed-T sampling or inference claim for hat_beta_N.
node.proof_tex:
Proof. Part I: exact primitive frontier. All h_{gt}, R_g, C_t, and M are strictly positive. Since q_{gt}=pi_g/T and m_{gt}=h_{gt}/pi_g, constrain beta to zero and define

  mu^0_{gt}=R_g C_t/(pi_g M).

This array is representable by intercept, cohort, and time effects because

  log mu^0_{gt}=log(R_g/pi_g)+log C_t-log M.

It matches every nuisance margin. Indeed, using sum_t C_t=M and sum_g R_g=M,

  sum_t pi_g mu^0_{gt}=R_g=sum_t pi_g m_{gt},
  sum_g pi_g mu^0_{gt}=C_t=sum_g pi_g m_{gt}.

After division by T these are exactly all cohort, time, and intercept score equations. The nuisance block is full rank as a principal block of the full collapsed Gram matrix, so this conditional fit is unique. Let theta^0=(eta^0,0) denote its normalized coefficient. Its only possibly nonzero score is the treatment coordinate

  s_0=sum_{g,t}q_{gt}D_{gt}(m_{gt}-mu^0_{gt})
     =(1/T){A-(1/M)sum_{g,t}D_{gt}R_gC_t}
     =Phi/(TM).

No pseudo-true nuisance multiplier remains in this expression. The Hessian of L is

  nabla^2 L(theta)=-sum_{g,t}q_{gt}exp(r_{gt}'theta)r_{gt}r_{gt}',

which is negative definite under ass:collapsed-design-rank. Hence L is strictly concave and its gradient is strictly monotone: for distinct theta_1 and theta_0,

  (theta_1-theta_0)'[nabla L(theta_1)-nabla L(theta_0)]<0.

If s_0=0, theta^0 is stationary in every coordinate and strict concavity makes it theta_star, so beta_star=0. If s_0 is nonzero, theta_star differs from theta^0 and

  0>(theta_star-theta^0)'[nabla L(theta_star)-nabla L(theta^0)]
   =-beta_star s_0,

because nabla L(theta_star)=0 and the nuisance coordinates of nabla L(theta^0) vanish. Thus beta_star s_0>0. Conversely, beta_star=0 with s_0 nonzero would contradict the same strict inequality. Since TM>0,

  sign{beta_star(delta)}=sign(s_0)=sign(Phi).

Phi is a finite explicit expression in pi_g, B_{gt}, and exp(D_gt delta_gt). Equality Phi=0 is equivalent to the two weak inequalities Phi>=0 and -Phi>=0. Intersecting Phi<0 with the primitive restrictions in def:global-sign-reversal-region gives exactly R_T. By lem:unit-fe-collapse this is a frontier for the limiting naive individual-unit-and-time-FE PPML coefficient, not merely for a relabelled cohort regression.

Part II: operational T=4 threshold. In the equal-share, unit-baseline family from the statement, the observed mean matrix, in row order (2,3,4,infinity) and column order (1,2,3,4), is

  [[1,x,x,y],[1,1,x,x],[1,1,1,x],[1,1,1,1]].

Its unweighted row margins are (1+2x+y,2+2x,3+x,4), its unweighted column margins are (4,3+x,2+2x,1+2x+y), its total is 10+5x+y, and its treated total is 5x+y. Direct expansion gives

  sum_{g,t}D_{gt}R_gC_t
   =20x^2+10xy+38x+y^2+12y+15

after removal of the common share factors. Because pi_g=1/4, every product in Phi carries the common factor 1/16. Therefore

  Phi=[(10+5x+y)(5x+y)
       -(20x^2+10xy+38x+y^2+12y+15)]/16
      =(5x^2+12x-2y-15)/16.

Part I yields beta_star<0 exactly when

  y>(5x^2+12x-15)/2,

and equality is the exact zero frontier. At x=101/100 this cutoff is 4441/4000. Moreover

  (5x^2+12x-15)/2-x=(5/2)(x-1)(x+3)>0

for x>1, so reversal forces the exceptional multiplier to be uniquely largest. W4 takes x=101/100 and y=4, for which Phi=-11559/32000<0; thus its witness is valid but nonminimal.

Part III: diagnose, then target causal PTT. Let H={(g,t):D_{gt}=1}. Since every adoption cohort belongs to {2,...,T,infinity}, D_{g1}=0. The period-one normalization and the limiting untreated mean give

  m_{g1}=B_{g1}=bar b_g.

The never-treated cohort obeys D_{infinity,t}=0 for all t, so

  m_{infinity,t}=bar b_infinity exp(gamma_{t0}),
  m_{infinity,1}=bar b_infinity>0.

Consequently the observed-population counterfactual baseline

  Bobs_{gt}=m_{g1}m_{infinity,t}/m_{infinity,1}

equals bar b_g exp(gamma_{t0})=B_{gt}. In a treated cell, proportional effects give m_{gt}=B_{gt}exp(delta_{gt}); hence

  tau_{gt}=m_{gt}/Bobs_{gt}-1=exp(delta_{gt})-1.

Positive cohort shares and baselines imply Z=sum_H q_{gt}Bobs_{gt}>0, omega_{gt}=q_{gt}Bobs_{gt}/Z>0, and sum_H omega_{gt}=1. Therefore

  PTT=sum_H omega_{gt}tau_{gt}
     ={sum_H q_{gt}B_{gt}exp(delta_{gt})}/{sum_H q_{gt}B_{gt}}-1
     ={sum_H q_{gt}m_{gt}}/{sum_H q_{gt}Bobs_{gt}}-1.

This is a computable positive-counterfactual-share aggregation of the identified granular proportional effects. If every delta_{gt}>0 on H, then every tau_{gt}>0 and PTT>0. Part I says Phi<0 exactly when beta_star<0. Hence Phi<0 diagnoses the pooled PPML coefficient as an unsafe causal summary: the recommended granular effects and PTT target a distinct causal estimand and do not algebraically correct or reinterpret beta_star. Everything here is an observed-population statement; no fixed-T sampling limit, influence function, variance formula, or coverage result for hat_beta_N is asserted. QED.
node.justification: The theorem closes the primitive-frontier question by proving sign(beta_star)=sign(Phi), eliminating all pseudo-true intercept, cohort, and time multipliers, and deriving the exact T=4 threshold and the qualified diagnose-then-causal-PTT rule.
node.gap: Published nonlinear DiD and PPML work does not give a finite necessary-and-sufficient primitive sign system for the misspecified pooled unit-and-time-FE PPML coefficient in a general fixed-T multicohort panel.
node.consumer: Design-stage users can evaluate Phi directly from cohort shares, baseline masses, and effects; if Phi<0, they should treat beta_star as an unsafe summary and report granular proportional effects or their distinct counterfactual-share PTT instead.

oeq:primitive-global-frontier.source_fingerprint: {"kind":"openendedquestion","statement":"For every T>=4 and every adoption support C satisfying ass:multicohort-frontier-scope, can the zero-level set {((pi_g),(B_gt),(delta_gt)): beta_star(delta)=0} associated with R_T be characterized by a finite necessary-and-sufficient system of inequalities in the primitive limiting timing shares pi_g=lim_N n_gN/N, baseline masses B_gt, and the full positive effect vector (delta_gt)_{D_gt=1}, without retaining implicit pseudo-true intercept, cohort, or time multipliers?","depends_on":["def:global-sign-reversal-region","def:frontier-elimination-handle","lem:unit-fe-collapse","lem:pseudo-true-ppml-projection","thm:sharp-ppml-forbidden-sign","ass:multicohort-frontier-scope","ass:strict-positive-effects"],"justification":"The fitted-residual iff rule diagnoses a realized panel, but a primitive zero-level characterization would reveal how timing, baselines, and heterogeneous effects jointly permit a unit-FE PPML sign reversal.","gap":"GoodmanBacon2021 gives linear comparison weights and deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuille2020TWFE characterizes negative linear weights; neither eliminates endogenous Poisson margins. MoreauKastler2025PTT §3.1 is a 2x3 calculation, not a finite T>=4 multicohort primitive zero-level characterization.","consumer":"A solution would provide design-stage screening rules for cohort composition and baseline imbalance in gravity, tax, and health panels using PPML."}
